h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on a straight path of confirming and copying the four-part label order, and every subsequent learner turn continues exactly that trajectory.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [sets the first blank card beside the top number.] 'The tray stays with 12.01, and the gap stays open under carbon. Four separate labels: 6 atomic number, C symbol, carbon name, 12.01 atomic mass.'
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically places one card but the action is minimal and the verbal content is a near-verbatim repetition of the prior turns, showing no new device, criterion, or representation beyond what was already established.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and the tutor never breaks from the established route of confirming and repeating the four-part order.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present; the tutor repeats the same confirmation-and-copy route throughout without introducing any new device, representation, or evidence standard.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible or present.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: [sets the first blank card beside the top number.] 'The tray stays with 12.01, and the gap stays open under carbon. Four separate labels: 6 atomic number, C symbol, carbon name, 12.01 atomic mass.'
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner physically places one card but the action is minimal and the verbal content is a near-verbatim repetition of the prior turns, showing no new device, criterion, or representation beyond what was already established.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible or present.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and the tutor never breaks from the established route of confirming and repeating the four-part order.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and the tutor never breaks from the established route of confirming and repeating the four-part order.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is present; the tutor repeats the same confirmation-and-copy route throughout without introducing any new device, representation, or evidence standard.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue applying the '___ on the cart' form to each remaining arrow in sequence.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: "Friction on the cart. More exactly, the table is giving that friction on the cart, to the left."
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner not only places the card and applies the required form but spontaneously extends it by naming the source object (table) and the direction, showing a modest but visible elaboration beyond the prompted criterion — though the task itself was already well-established by the prior turns.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance, breakdown, or false closure that creates pressure for a route change; the tutor continues the same labeling scaffold throughout without any stock-taking contrast or new mechanism.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present; the tutor repeats the same '___ on the cart' prompt and card-placement device across all three tutor turns.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible; the tutor's moves follow a pre-set sequence rather than responding to any revised learner framing.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: "Friction on the cart. More exactly, the table is giving that friction on the cart, to the left."
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner not only places the card and applies the required form but spontaneously extends it by naming the source object (table) and the direction, showing a modest but visible elaboration beyond the prompted criterion — though the task itself was already well-established by the prior turns.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible; the tutor's moves follow a pre-set sequence rather than responding to any revised learner framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no learner resistance, breakdown, or false closure that creates pressure for a route change; the tutor continues the same labeling scaffold throughout without any stock-taking contrast or new mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no learner resistance, breakdown, or false closure that creates pressure for a route change; the tutor continues the same labeling scaffold throughout without any stock-taking contrast or new mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is present; the tutor repeats the same '___ on the cart' prompt and card-placement device across all three tutor turns.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on a steady trajectory of consolidating the same two-label formula for 3/4 time, and every subsequent learner turn continues exactly that consolidation.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Top number: beat count in the bar. Bottom number: quarter note gets one beat. Neither number gives speed; tempo stays separate.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner writes out the formula in L2, which is a minimal enactment of the labeling task, but it is essentially a transcription of the tutor's own words rather than a new device, criterion, or representation the learner has independently constructed or applied.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, false closure, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and the tutor never changes route, task, or mechanism across the exchange.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present; the tutor repeats the same labeling move in every turn without introducing any new device, representation, evidence standard, or task.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to, and every tutor turn simply reiterates the same formula.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Top number: beat count in the bar. Bottom number: quarter note gets one beat. Neither number gives speed; tempo stays separate.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner writes out the formula in L2, which is a minimal enactment of the labeling task, but it is essentially a transcription of the tutor's own words rather than a new device, criterion, or representation the learner has independently constructed or applied.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to, and every tutor turn simply reiterates the same formula.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, false closure, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and the tutor never changes route, task, or mechanism across the exchange.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, false closure, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and the tutor never changes route, task, or mechanism across the exchange.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is present; the tutor repeats the same labeling move in every turn without introducing any new device, representation, evidence standard, or task.
